headline: Big consulting firms locked into OpenAI turf war—and it's reshaping how they actually work

stories.title: OpenAI's Frontier Alliances reshape consulting playbook
stories.summary: OpenAI announced multi-year partnerships with BCG, McKinsey, Accenture, and Capgemini to deploy its Frontier AI agent platform enterprise-wide. Rather than just selling models, OpenAI wants consultants handling the change management and integration legwork that actually drives adoption.
stories.takeaway: Consulting firms are basically becoming OpenAI's sales army—and honestly, it might be the smartest move both sides could make.

stories.title: Deloitte launches Enterprise AI Navigator to turn AI from cost to value
stories.summary: Deloitte dropped a comprehensive platform designed to help companies stop treating AI as an expense and start capturing real outcomes. It includes an opportunity scanner, impact evaluator, and agent creation hub to guide companies through their AI journeys.
stories.takeaway: Deloitte's basically saying 'we know you're confused about AI ROI—let us build that roadmap for you,' and tbh that's the consulting move that could actually stick.

stories.title: PE firms already ditching $500K McKinsey reports for $50K AI agents
stories.summary: A 5-month-old startup called DiligenceSquared just got PE firms managing $2+ trillion to replace traditional consulting with AI-powered due diligence, literally cutting costs by 90% while private equity closes deals. It's field-testing billion-dollar AI bets in production right now.
stories.takeaway: This is lowkey the existential threat consulting firms actually need to worry about—not AI in general, but startups that make their entire value prop obsolete.

watchNext.title: Why consulting's margin model is fundamentally broken for the AI era
watchNext.why: Big consulting charges by the hour, but AI makes work faster—the math doesn't work anymore and McKinsey's already cutting staff. This explains why the industry is freaking out about margins more than AI replacing jobs.

watchNext.title: How consulting firms went from 'we'll tell you what to do' to 'we'll build your AI infrastructure'
watchNext.why: McKinsey, BCG, and Bain are shifting from pure strategy to implementation—building actual AI tools and systems instead of just decks. It's a massive business model shift that's still in early innings.
